problem.passage:
5. Read Ms. Lee’s opinions about the grammar lesson in <A> and
the sample lesson plan in <B>, and follow the directions.
【4 points】
<A>
I think teachers should keep in mind that the ultimate goal
of any grammar lesson is to build up communicative ability.
In order to achieve this goal, I believe that classroom
activities should not focus on practicing structures and
patterns in a meaningless way. Instead, they should be
designed to involve students in real communication. By
doing so, grammar lessons will be able to encourage the
students’ interest in learning and elicit more active and
meaningful interaction with others in the classroom.
<B>
High School
1st-year Subject Students students
English
Lesson 9
Title Date Nov. 24th
My Dream
∙Students will familiarize themselves with the
expression “If I were ... .” Objec-
tives ∙Students will be able to communicate using
the expression “If I were ... .”
Teaching-Learning Activities
∙T and Ss exchange greetings. Greeting &
Roll-call ∙T checks if all the Ss are present.
∙T reviews materials from the Introduc-
Review
tion previous lesson.
∙T introduces the objective of the Stating the
Objectives lesson.
∙T hands out a text that contains
several instances of “If I were ... .”
∙Ss scan the text and highlight all
the sentences including “If I
were ... .” Activity 1
∙Ss check the ones they highlighted
with T.
∙T tells Ss to pay attention to the
Develop-
verb form “were.”
ment
∙T tells Ss that she is going to
read a passage on “My Dream.”
∙T explains difficult words in the
passage.
Activity 2
∙T reads the passage at a normal
pace.
∙Ss jot down the key words in the
passage as T reads.
영 어 [전공B]
∙Ss reconstruct the passage
individually.
∙T hands out the original text to
Ss.
∙T has Ss form groups of three.
∙T asks Ss to think of a job that
they would like to have in the
future.
∙Ss use “If I were ... ” to share
their opinions about their future
dream jobs.
Activity 3
∙Assuming that their dreams come
true, two Ss take a reporter’s role
and interview the other S asking
how he or she feels about his or
her job.
∙Ss take turns and continue the
activity.
∙T hands out a worksheet.
∙Ss put together sentence fragments
to form complete sentences.
∙T reads out complete sentences
Activity 4 and each S checks their own
answers.
∙T writes three more sentences
using “If I were ... ” on the board.
∙T asks Ss to read the sentences.
Review ∙T reviews what Ss learned.
Consoli- ∙T hands out homework and
dation
Closure announces the next lesson.
∙T says goodbye to Ss.
Note: T=teacher, S=student
Based on <A>, choose the ONE most appropriate activity in the
development stage that reflects Ms. Lee’s opinions. Then,
support your choice with evidence from <B>. Do NOT copy
more than FOUR consecutive words from the passage.
4
